project.name: PropManager Phase 3 Audit
project.description: Defensive code audit covering multi-tenant authorization scope leakage and N+1/performance hotspots. Phase 1 (commit 03e17b7) covered 32 SQLi/auth/serialization/file-upload bugs; Phase 2 (commit 4a305a0) covered 17 data-integrity bugs. Phase 3 surfaces 45 findings across tenant-isolation and runtime performance — different bug class, equally costly when missed.

techStack.testing: PHPUnit 11.5 via ParaTest 7.8 (--parallel), Dusk for browser E2E

qualityStandards.description: Phase 3 fixes must preserve Phase 1+2 contracts: TenantScope semantics, InvoiceStatus enum migration, encrypted credential casts, audit logging.
principles: Every TenantScope bypass (withoutGlobalScope, withoutGlobalScopes, DB::table) requires either an explicit landlord_id filter OR a documented architectural reason (e.g. token-based public lookup)
Every model with a landlord_id column SHOULD use the TenantScope trait as defense-in-depth, even if all current callers filter manually
Every API route returning model data must enforce ability:* middleware AND a Form Request authorize() check, not just one or the other
Every controller method calling Model::all() or ::get() on hot paths must declare its eager loads — the codebase already has Lazy Loading violation detection in non-prod
Every Inertia::render passing a paginated or large collection must specify select() columns to avoid loading wide rows
Every scheduled job (routes/console.php) iterating a collection must verify N+1 absence before deploy

commands.find_n_plus_1: Use Laravel's Model::preventLazyLoading() in AppServiceProvider::boot() during dev to surface lazy loads as exceptions

tenant_scope_critical.description: Confirmed cross-tenant data exposure or write paths. Fix before anything else.

performance_critical.description: Hot-path N+1 or unbounded collection loads — measurable user-facing slowdown or job-runner scale failure.

tenant_scope_defense.description: Scope is bypassed but a manual filter exists. Functionally safe; fragile to future refactors.

performance_optimization.description: Real N+1 or over-fetching on cool paths, or missing caches/indexes worth picking up.

cleanup_consistency.description: Inconsistencies with codebase conventions but not exploitable. Pick up when touching the area.

items.title: Bulk invoice generation iterates ALL tenants' leases in CLI context
items.problem: TenantScope::bootTenantScope skips scope registration when Auth::check() is false (CLI / queue / cron). The `invoices:generate` artisan command and InvoiceAutomationService call Lease::where('is_active', true)->get() with no landlord_id constraint, so a single cron run iterates every active lease across every landlord and writes invoices for tenants who didn't request them.
items.evidence: grep -n "Lease::where('is_active', true)" -- 3 hits, none with landlord_id filter. TenantScope.php:41 confirms `if (Auth::check() && !Auth::user()->isSuperAdmin())` — no scope when no auth.
items.fix: Refactor GenerateMonthlyInvoices::handle to chunk by landlord: `User::landlords()->each(fn($l) => $this->generateForLandlord($l->id))`. In each landlord pass, use `Lease::withoutGlobalScope('landlord')->where('landlord_id', $l->id)->where('is_active', true)`. Add a regression test that asserts an unauthenticated artisan call only touches one specified landlord's leases.
items.blast_radius: Cron-triggered duplicate/erroneous invoices for every landlord on the platform; potential billing chaos and disputed charges.

items.title: Mpesa STK push and status endpoints accept any tenant's invoice ID
items.problem: `POST /api/v1/mpesa/stk-push` and the related status endpoint sit outside the `ability:tenant:read` middleware group used by /tenant/payments routes. The controller calls `Invoice::findOrFail($request->invoice_id)` with no ownership check. Authenticated landlord/caretaker tokens can invoke STK push against arbitrary invoice IDs.
items.evidence: routes/api.php:111: `Route::post('/mpesa/stk-push', [MpesaController::class, 'initiateStkPush'])->middleware(['auth:sanctum', 'throttle:payment'])` — no ability check. Compare with /tenant/payments/mpesa/initiate which is wrapped with ability:tenant:read.
items.fix: Move both routes into the `ability:tenant:read` group. Add explicit ownership guard in MpesaController::checkStatus: `if ($invoice->lease->tenant_id !== $request->user()->id) abort(403)`. Audit InitiateMpesaPaymentRequest::authorize() for parity.
items.blast_radius: Any authenticated API user triggers STK pushes on invoices belonging to other tenants; potentially used for harassment or to charge wrong M-Pesa numbers.

items.title: DocumentController index/store use auth()->id() — broken for caretakers
items.problem: DocumentController::index filters Document::where('landlord_id', auth()->id()). For a caretaker, auth()->id() returns the caretaker's user ID — not their landlord's. Index returns zero documents. store() assigns landlord_id = auth()->id() (caretaker ID), creating orphaned documents. Download/view paths at lines 178/216 correctly use `$user->landlord_id`, so the inconsistency is index/store-only.
items.evidence: DocumentController.php:24: `Document::where('landlord_id', auth()->id())`. Compare DocumentController.php:178: `if ($user->role === 'caretaker' && $document->landlord_id !== $user->landlord_id)` — correct caretaker-aware check.
items.fix: Add `$landlordId = $user->isCaretaker() ? $user->landlord_id : $user->id;` resolution at the top of every DocumentController action. Replace all `auth()->id()` references with `$landlordId`.
items.blast_radius: Caretakers see zero documents (DoS for them); their uploads are orphaned to a landlord_id that doesn't exist as a landlord.

items.title: ReconciliationReport model has landlord_id but no TenantScope trait
items.problem: ReconciliationReport stores per-landlord Paystack reconciliation data (payment discrepancies, dispute metadata) but does not use the TenantScope trait. Current callers filter manually via where('landlord_id'). Any future code path calling ReconciliationReport::query() without that filter exposes all landlords' reconciliation results.
items.evidence: Model declaration: only `use HasFactory`. Migration shows landlord_id as a foreignId.
items.fix: Add `use App\Traits\TenantScope;` and `use TenantScope;` to ReconciliationReport. Run the test suite — manual filters become redundant but safe.
items.blast_radius: Defense-in-depth gap. No active leak today; any future call site without an explicit filter would expose all landlords' reconciliation data.

items.title: BankReconciliationQueue model has landlord_id but no TenantScope trait
items.problem: BankReconciliationQueue stores per-landlord bank-statement matching records but omits TenantScope. PaymentArchivalService at line 120 uses raw DB::table('bank_reconciliation_queue')->where('payment_id', ...)->delete() — scoped only by payment_id with no landlord_id filter. Cross-tenant protection relies entirely on the payment_id never collide-leaking; an enumeration bug elsewhere would cascade.
items.evidence: Model declaration: only `use HasFactory`. Service uses raw DB::table() instead of Eloquent.
items.fix: Add `use App\Traits\TenantScope;` to BankReconciliationQueue. Refactor PaymentArchivalService to use Eloquent (`BankReconciliationQueue::where('payment_id', ...)->delete()`) so the scope kicks in.
items.blast_radius: Same as SCOPE-S4 — defense-in-depth.

items.title: /api/v1/integrations/reports endpoints scope to super-admin's empty tenant
items.problem: Integration reports are gated by `ability:integration:webhook`, granted only to super admins. Inside the controller, $landlordId = $user->isCaretaker() ? $user->landlord_id : $user->id — for a super admin this is their own user ID (a non-landlord), returning empty data. There's no validation preventing a `?landlord_id=X` query parameter override, inviting a future request-shape bug to escalate.
items.fix: Require an explicit `landlord_id` query parameter on integration routes with FormRequest validation. Validate the super-admin token has scoped access. If integration tokens should be cross-tenant, document and add explicit cross-tenant flag.
items.blast_radius: Logic error today (returns empty); design invites privilege escalation if /integrations/reports starts accepting landlord_id input without validation.

items.title: Landlord dashboard fires 4 separate aging queries per render (and 4 more in real-time path)
items.problem: calculateLandlordMetrics() calls getArrearsInRangeForLeases() four times with different day bounds. Each issues an independent SUM(total_due - amount_paid) over the same lease set. The pattern repeats in calculateQuickMetrics() which fires on every payment broadcast event.
items.evidence: Lines 513-518 show `arrearsAging = ['0_30', '31_60', '61_90', '90_plus']` each as a separate call. FinanceStatsService::getArrearsStats already implements the single-query CASE-WHEN version.
items.fix: Replace all four calls with one selectRaw query: `Invoice::whereIn('lease_id', $leaseIds)->selectRaw("SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(NOW(), due_date) BETWEEN 0 AND 30 THEN total_due - amount_paid ELSE 0 END) as bucket_0_30, SUM(CASE WHEN ... BETWEEN 31 AND 60 ... ) as bucket_31_60, ...")->first()`. Match the FinanceStatsService::getArrearsStats pattern (lines 151-186).
items.estimated_impact: 3 extra queries eliminated per landlord dashboard render, plus 3 more on every payment broadcast (Reverb event). High-volume landlords see this fire dozens of times per minute.

items.title: Caretaker dashboard fires 5 redundant ticket COUNT queries
items.problem: getCaretakerDashboardData() runs 5 separate COUNT queries against tickets, each with the same `assigned_to = $caretaker->id` predicate, differing only by status/priority filter combinations.
items.evidence: Lines 226-243 show actionItems[urgent_tickets|open_tickets] and ticketStats[total|open|urgent|resolved] all firing as independent counts.
items.fix: Consolidate to one selectRaw: `Ticket::where('assigned_to', $caretaker->id)->selectRaw("COUNT(*) as total, COUNT(CASE WHEN status NOT IN ('resolved','closed') THEN 1 END) as open, COUNT(CASE WHEN status NOT IN ('resolved','closed') AND priority='urgent' THEN 1 END) as urgent_open, COUNT(CASE WHEN status='resolved' THEN 1 END) as resolved")->first()`.
items.estimated_impact: 4 queries eliminated per caretaker dashboard render.

items.title: InvoiceController::generate loads ALL active leases across ALL landlords
items.problem: Lease::where('is_active', true)->with(['unit.building.property', 'tenant'])->get() — no landlord_id filter (TenantScope active for the auth context but the iteration loops to invoke generateInvoiceForLease for each lease). On a 1,000-lease platform, hydrates 1,000 leases with 4 nested eager loads in a single synchronous request. Same root cause as SCOPE-S1; address jointly.
items.fix: Add `->where('landlord_id', auth()->id())` to scope. Convert to chunked iteration (`->chunkById(100, fn($chunk) => ...)`) and dispatch generateInvoiceForLease as a queued job to avoid request timeouts. Coordinate fix with SCOPE-S1.
items.estimated_impact: Memory/timeout risk: unbounded collection load. Synchronous N writes of generated invoices in a single HTTP request.

items.title: BulkRentAdjuster fires one Lease query per lease ID inside the loop
items.problem: execute() loops over $this->leaseIds and calls processLease($leaseId) for each, which issues `Lease::where('id', $leaseId)->...->firstOrFail()`. A 100-lease batch = 100 individual SELECTs inside one DB transaction.
items.fix: Pre-load all target leases keyed by ID before the loop: `$leases = Lease::whereIn('id', $this->leaseIds)->where('landlord_id', $this->landlordId)->where('is_active', true)->with('tenant:id,name')->get()->keyBy('id');`. Inside the loop, look up `$leases[$leaseId] ?? throw new ModelNotFoundException(...)`.
items.estimated_impact: Reduces from 1+N queries to 1 pre-load. For N=100 leases that's 99 queries eliminated per bulk rent adjustment.

items.title: SchedulerService::processRentReminders — N+1 lease query per tenant every 5 min
items.problem: getEligibleTenants() returns Users with no relations. The loop calls `$tenant->leases()->where('is_active', true)->first()` per tenant. For a landlord with 200 tenants this fires 200 queries every 5 minutes.
items.evidence: Line 93-95: `foreach ($tenants as $tenant) { $lease = $tenant->leases()->where('is_active', true)->first(); ... }`. Line 235: `User::...->get()` with no `with()`.
items.fix: Add eager-load to getEligibleTenants(): `->with(['leases' => fn($q) => $q->where('is_active', true)->with('unit.building.property')])`. Inside loop: `$lease = $tenant->leases->first()` (pre-loaded).
items.estimated_impact: Reduces 1+N queries per scheduled run to 2 total (1 tenant load + 1 lease eager load). Runs every 5 minutes.

items.title: SchedulerService::processArrearsNotices — same N+1 pattern as P5
items.problem: Same shape as PERF-P5: getTenatsWithArrears() returns Users without preloaded relations; loop fires per-tenant `$tenant->leases()->where('is_active', true)->where('arrears', '>', 0)->first()`.
items.fix: Eager-load filtered relation: `->with(['leases' => fn($q) => $q->where('is_active', true)->where('arrears', '>', 0)])`.
items.estimated_impact: Eliminates up to 200 queries per scheduled run; pairs with P5 — fix together.

items.title: DashboardService::getTenantKycStats — hasCompletedKyc() runs 2 queries per tenant
items.problem: `$tenants->filter(fn($t) => $t->hasCompletedKyc())` calls hasCompletedKyc() per tenant. The accessor lazy-loads $this->lease, then queries KycRequirement, then counts kycSubmissions. For a 50-tenant building this is 100+ extra queries per dashboard.
items.evidence: Line 689-690: `$complete = $tenants->filter(fn($t) => $t->hasCompletedKyc())->count();`. User::hasCompletedKyc at User.php:147 issues 2 queries.
items.fix: Replace with a single DB aggregate: join kyc_submissions on user_id grouped by user_id, compare COUNT(verified) >= COUNT(required). Or use `kyc_completed_at IS NOT NULL` as a fast proxy if the column is reliably maintained.
items.estimated_impact: 2N queries eliminated per dashboard render where N = tenants in the building (significant on 50-100 tenant buildings).

items.title: PaymentHealthService loads all tenants' encrypted payment credentials on unauth health check
items.problem: PaymentHealthService::loadConfigurations() uses `withoutGlobalScopes()` to fetch all PaymentConfiguration rows including encrypted secret keys for all landlords. Called by HealthCheckController::payments() which is wrapped only in `throttle:api` — no auth. The secrets aren't returned in JSON, but they're hydrated into memory on every health-check call from any unauthenticated client.
items.fix: Either require auth + ability:admin on /health/payments, OR narrow loadConfigurations to fetch only non-secret fields needed for the ping (e.g., `paystack_enabled`, `mpesa_environment`). Don't hydrate secret_key columns at all unless needed.
items.blast_radius: No active leak via the JSON response. Compounding risk: a future serializer change or debug toggle would expose every landlord's payment credentials publicly.

items.title: FinanceStatsService::getExpenseStats uses withoutGlobalScopes with manual filter — fragile
items.problem: Expense::withoutGlobalScopes()->where('expenses.landlord_id', $landlordId) — functionally safe, but bypasses TenantScope wholesale. If $landlordId is ever sourced from untrusted input, exposure is direct.
items.fix: Replace `withoutGlobalScopes()` with `withoutGlobalScope('landlord')` (named bypass — preserves any other future scopes). Add a code comment naming the manual filter as the security control.
items.blast_radius: If a future caller mis-routes user input as $landlordId, all tenants' expense data leaks.

items.title: PaymentLinkService::revokeForInvoice and cleanupExpired bypass scope
items.problem: revokeForInvoice($invoiceId) and cleanupExpired() use `withoutGlobalScope('landlord')` with only an invoice_id or expires_at filter. revokeForInvoice in particular is an IDOR if a tenant passes an invoice ID belonging to another tenant.
items.fix: In revokeForInvoice, add `->whereHas('invoice', fn($q) => $q->where('landlord_id', $landlordId))` or accept landlord_id as a parameter. In cleanupExpired, the cross-tenant nature is intentional — add a code comment.
items.blast_radius: Possible cross-tenant payment-link revocation via ID enumeration.

items.title: DailyPaymentReconciliation depends on remembering the landlord_id filter on every query
items.problem: Pattern is correct (uses `withoutGlobalScope('landlord')->where('landlord_id', $specificId)`), but the entire chain relies on developer discipline to add `where('landlord_id', ...)` on every query. One forgotten filter = silent cross-tenant exposure in a financial reconciliation flow.
items.fix: Encapsulate the per-landlord query path in a repository method (e.g., `PaymentRepository::forLandlord($id)->reconcilable()`) so the landlord_id filter is unforgettable by construction.
items.blast_radius: Today: safe. Future: any new query in this chain without filter exposes all landlords' reconciliation data.

items.title: ArchiveOldPayments — audit log entries may have null landlord_id
items.problem: PaymentArchivalService::archivePayment calls AuditLog::withoutGlobalScope('landlord')->create([...]) without populating landlord_id. The job runs as a queue worker (no Auth) so TenantScope's creating hook doesn't auto-fill landlord_id. Result: archival audit logs carry null landlord_id and are invisible to landlords reviewing their audit trail.
items.fix: In archivePayment, explicitly include `'landlord_id' => $payment->landlord_id` in the AuditLog::create payload. Add a regression test that archives a payment in a queue context and verifies the audit log row has the correct landlord_id.
items.blast_radius: Compliance gap: landlords miss critical archival events in their audit log review.

items.title: HandleInertiaRequests landlord nav badges rely solely on TenantScope
items.problem: Badge counts (`TenantMessage::where('status', ...)->count()`, `Invoice::where('status', 'overdue')->count()`, `Ticket::open()->count()`) rely entirely on the global TenantScope. If TenantScope is ever bypassed by a refactor or runs in a context where Auth::check() unexpectedly returns false (impersonation, queue), these badge counts leak system-wide totals.
items.fix: Add explicit `->where('landlord_id', $user->id)` to all badge count queries in the landlord branch as defense-in-depth alongside TenantScope. Match the redundant-filter pattern used elsewhere.
items.blast_radius: Currently safe. Any TenantScope regression cascades to nav UI.

items.title: AuditLogController::forModel allows model-existence enumeration across tenants
items.problem: AuditLogController::forModel accepts model_type + model_id from user input. For non-super-admins, it filters audit log results by landlord_id, but it does not first verify that the requested model belongs to the landlord. A landlord can probe arbitrary model IDs and infer their existence from empty-vs-populated audit log responses.
items.fix: Resolve $model from $modelType/$modelId before querying audit logs. Verify $model->landlord_id === $landlordId. Return 404 if not found or not owned. Then query audit logs.
items.blast_radius: Cross-tenant model ID enumeration via timing/empty-response side channel.

items.title: ArrearsController fires 7 redundant aggregate queries per page load
items.problem: After paginating, the controller computes a $stats array (3 separate queries: COUNT DISTINCT lease_id, COUNT, SUM) and a $aging array (4 SUM queries — same scan, different date ranges). All 7 hit the same WHERE-predicated rows. FinanceStatsService::getArrearsStats already implements the consolidated single-query version.
items.fix: Replace the 7 queries with `FinanceCacheService::rememberStats('arrears', $landlordId, fn() => $this->financeStatsService->getArrearsStats($landlordId))`. Map the result keys to existing $stats/$aging shape.
items.estimated_impact: 6 queries eliminated per arrears page load + caching benefit on repeat hits.

items.title: NotificationsController::sendRentReminders dispatches one job per tenant
items.problem: Loads full Lease collection with `with('tenant:id,name')->get()` (no select for Lease columns), then dispatches a SendNotificationJob per tenant. For a landlord with 500 active leases, this fans out 500 individual queue jobs.
items.fix: Add `->select(['id', 'tenant_id', 'rent_amount', 'landlord_id'])` on the Lease query. Replace per-tenant dispatch with a single SendBulkNotificationsJob carrying the lease ID list.
items.estimated_impact: Reduces queue fan-out from N jobs to 1; reduces memory by skipping unused Lease columns.

items.title: ReportService::getOccupancyMetrics loads all units for PHP-side aggregation
items.problem: Unit::where('landlord_id', $landlordId)->get() pulls all unit rows, then PHP aggregates with `$units->where('status', 'occupied')->count()` etc. The Api/ReportController::occupancy already does this correctly with `selectRaw('status, COUNT(*) as cnt')->groupBy('status')->pluck('cnt', 'status')`.
items.fix: Mirror the Api/ReportController::occupancy implementation: single groupBy('status') aggregate.
items.estimated_impact: Eliminates loading N unit rows. For 200 units, ~200 row hydrations skipped per render.

items.title: LeaseController::batchAdjustRent sends mail synchronously per lease
items.problem: `Mail::to($lease->tenant)->send(new RentHikeNotice(...))` inside a foreach. For a 30-unit batch, that's 30 synchronous SMTP calls in one HTTP request — typically 200-2000ms each.
items.fix: Replace `->send(...)` with `->queue(...)`. Or better, dispatch SendNotificationJob (which already supports email + SMS + WhatsApp fallback channels).
items.estimated_impact: Eliminates 6-60 seconds of synchronous SMTP latency from a single HTTP request.

items.title: TenantController::show loads full Invoice/Payment rows for short summary panels
items.problem: `Payment::where('lease_id', ...)->limit(10)->get()` and `Invoice::where('lease_id', ...)->limit(10)->get()` fetch full model rows. The view only renders ~4 fields per row.
items.fix: Add explicit `->select(['id', 'amount', 'payment_method', 'payment_date', 'reference'])` for payments and `->select(['id', 'invoice_number', 'total_due', 'amount_paid', 'status', 'due_date'])` for invoices.
items.estimated_impact: Reduces row-bytes per Inertia render — meaningful on encrypted PII columns we don't need.

items.title: TenantsHubController verifications list eager-loads full tenant rows including encrypted PII
items.problem: TenantVerification->with('lease.tenant')->paginate(20) eager-loads entire User rows including encrypted national_id and bank_details. Verifications list only needs id+name+email.
items.fix: Use `->with(['lease:id,tenant_id', 'lease.tenant:id,name,email'])` to constrain columns.
items.estimated_impact: Avoids loading encrypted PII per row; reduces decryption overhead and memory.

items.title: DashboardService uses whereHas where whereIn would suffice
items.problem: Two queries use `Payment::whereHas('lease', fn($q) => $q->whereIn('unit_id', $metricsUnitIds))` — translates to a correlated subquery (EXISTS). $metricsLeaseIds is already computed at line 475; using it directly in `whereIn('lease_id', $metricsLeaseIds)` is faster.
items.fix: Replace whereHas with `Payment::whereIn('lease_id', $metricsLeaseIds)`. Same fix in both call sites.
items.estimated_impact: Replaces correlated subquery with direct indexed IN clause.

items.title: Api/ReportController::arrears (v1) — full collection load + PHP-side bucketing
items.problem: v1 endpoint loads all overdue invoices `->with('lease.tenant', 'lease.unit', 'lease.unit.building')->get()`, then buckets in PHP. v2 endpoint (arrearsV2 at line 124) already implements this with selectRaw + cursor pagination — but mobile API still calls v1.
items.fix: Direct mobile clients to v2. Or apply v2's selectRaw + cursor pagination to v1 directly.
items.estimated_impact: For a landlord with 500 overdue invoices: 500 model hydrations + 3 nested eager loads avoided per API call.

items.title: PaymentsHubController::overview computes collection rate via 2 queries; not cached
items.problem: calculateCollectionRate fires 2 SUMs (total_due, amount_paid) on the same predicate. getPaymentStats just above fires 4 more uncached aggregate queries. None cached — every overview tab load re-runs all 6.
items.fix: Combine into one selectRaw('SUM(total_due) as invoiced, SUM(amount_paid) as collected'). Wrap getPaymentStats result in FinanceCacheService::rememberStats with appropriate TTL and invalidation.
items.estimated_impact: 1+ queries eliminated per render; caching eliminates all 6 on warm hits.

items.title: TenantsHubController::getCounts re-runs 4 COUNTs on every tab navigation
items.problem: getCounts() runs 4 COUNT queries on every tab nav (no cache). Tab switching is frequent on this page.
items.fix: Wrap in `Cache::remember("tenant_hub_counts:{$landlordId}", 60, fn() => [...])`. Invalidate via observer hooks on TenantInvitation, TenantVerification, TenantPaymentVerification, MoveOut.
items.estimated_impact: 3 queries skipped per repeat hub nav inside the 60s TTL.

items.title: FinanceNotificationController::sendArrearsNotices loads full collection just to count
items.problem: Invoice::where(...)->with('lease.tenant')->get() then `foreach ($invoices as $i) if ($i->lease?->tenant?->email) $count++` — full hydration with relations just for a count. The endpoint doesn't actually send anything; it returns the count as a flash message.
items.fix: Replace with `Invoice::where('landlord_id', $id)->where('status', InvoiceStatus::Overdue)->whereHas('lease.tenant', fn($q) => $q->whereNotNull('email'))->count()`.
items.estimated_impact: Eliminates full collection load for a no-op endpoint.

items.title: WarmFinanceCacheJob — getHubStats double-executes overview stats on cold cache
items.problem: getHubStats wraps in `rememberStats('hub', ...)` and inside calls `getOverviewStats()` (which has its own remember block). When both caches are cold (warm-up after invalidation), inner queries execute once for `overview` and again inside `hub`.
items.fix: In WarmFinanceCacheJob::handle, warm `overview` and `arrears` BEFORE warming `hub` so the nested calls find warm caches.
items.estimated_impact: Avoids double execution of overview's payment/invoice queries during cache warm-up.

items.title: WaterSettingsController uses auth()->id() — same caretaker bug as DocumentController
items.problem: PUT /water/settings called by a caretaker writes `PaymentConfiguration::updateOrCreate(['landlord_id' => $caretakerUserId], ...)`. Same root cause as SCOPE-S3.
items.fix: Same fix as SCOPE-S3: resolve `$landlordId = $user->isCaretaker() ? $user->landlord_id : $user->id;`. Add `$this->authorize('update', WaterSetting::class)` or middleware role guard.
items.blast_radius: Caretaker write creates an orphaned config row; same bug-class as SCOPE-S3.

items.title: MoveOutDeductionCategoryController — verify policy class matches AuthServiceProvider registration
items.problem: Recent untracked addition; verify `MoveOutDeductionCategoryPolicy::viewAny()` is correctly defined and respects landlord ownership for non-super-admins.
items.evidence: Line 21 calls authorize. Policy class needs review.
items.fix: Code review of MoveOutDeductionCategoryPolicy methods (viewAny, view, create, update, delete). Ensure landlord-scoped checks. No code change yet — verification only.
items.blast_radius: None known; flagged because the policy was added in this session's recovery commits.

items.title: KycRequirementPolicy may allow landlords to delete platform defaults
items.problem: KycRequirement.landlord_id IS NULL = platform default. Verify KycRequirementPolicy::delete() rejects deletion when landlord_id is null and user is not a super admin.
items.evidence: KycRequirement::scopeGlobal and ::isGlobal at lines 120 confirm landlord_id IS NULL semantics.
items.fix: In KycRequirementPolicy::delete, add `if ($kycRequirement->landlord_id === null) return $user->isSuperAdmin();`.
items.blast_radius: Landlord deletion of a platform default would affect all landlords' onboarding flows.

items.title: Refund / Import / LandlordPayoutAccount missing from AuthServiceProvider $policies
items.problem: These models have landlord_id and TenantScope but no registered Policy. Controllers rely on manual filter checks; no central authz audit trail; @can directives would silently return false.
items.evidence: Grep shows Refund, Import, LandlordPayoutAccount used in controllers; no Policy registration in AuthServiceProvider.
items.fix: Create Policy classes (RefundPolicy, ImportPolicy, LandlordPayoutAccountPolicy) and register in AuthServiceProvider::$policies. Update controllers to call `$this->authorize(...)`.
items.blast_radius: Authz layer gap; not exploitable today but invites future regressions.

items.title: SecurityLog pre-auth events have null landlord_id
items.problem: Failed-login security logs are created in unauthenticated context; TenantScope's creating hook skips landlord_id population. Result: log entries are visible only to super admins, not the landlord whose tenant attempted login.
items.evidence: TenantScope.php:69 creating hook gated on Auth::check().
items.fix: On pre-login failures, resolve target user from email and populate `landlord_id` from `$targetUser->landlord_id` (or `$targetUser->id` if landlord). Add a regression test.
items.blast_radius: Compliance/visibility gap on tenant brute-force events.

items.title: HandleInertiaRequests deferred props — convention compliant, document
items.problem: getPendingInvitations uses `withoutGlobalScope('landlord')` deliberately for tenant-self-lookup. Pattern is correct but deserves documentation.
items.fix: No code change. Add doc comment explaining why scope bypass is required (cross-tenant invitation lookup by user ID).
items.blast_radius: None — pattern is correct.

items.title: BulkOperationsController validates lease ownership via building chain, not direct landlord_id
items.problem: validateLeasesBelongToBuilding uses `whereHas('unit', fn($q) => $q->whereIn('building_id', $buildingIds))`. Correct in practice (because building_id is then verified against landlord_id). But if data corruption ever desyncs lease.landlord_id from unit.building.property.landlord_id, the check passes incorrectly.
items.fix: Add redundant `->where('landlord_id', $landlordId)` directly on the Lease query.
items.blast_radius: Only materializes with pre-existing data corruption.

items.title: User::getPlanAttribute lazy-loads subscription on every CheckPlanLimits middleware hit
items.problem: `$this->subscription?->plan ?? SubscriptionPlan::free()` lazy-loads subscription if not eager-loaded. CheckPlanLimits middleware calls $user->canAccessFeature() / withinLimit() / getLimit() — each accesses $this->plan, each may trigger a subscription query.
items.fix: In CheckPlanLimits middleware, call `$request->user()->loadMissing('subscription.plan')` before any plan check. Or memoize in the User model: `$this->loadedPlan ??= $this->subscription?->plan ?? SubscriptionPlan::free();`.
items.estimated_impact: 1 query per request on landlord-scoped routes when subscription not preloaded.

items.title: Lease::getVerificationProgressAttribute lazy-loads verifications relation
items.problem: Accessor accesses $this->verifications. TenantController::show eager-loads it, but TenantIndexService::getActiveTenants does not — hits N+1 on the index page if the attribute is serialized.
items.fix: Either eager-load 'verifications' wherever Lease is serialized for Inertia, or remove from $appends and compute on-demand only when explicitly requested.
items.estimated_impact: Up to 1 query per lease on hub index pages.

items.title: FinanceStatsService::calculateExpenseCategoryBreakdown accesses category relation per group
items.problem: `$group->first()->category?->name` triggers lazy load if category isn't eager-loaded on the source $expenses collection.
items.fix: Ensure $expenses is loaded with `->with('category:id,name,color')`. Or use the DB-level breakdown already in getExpenseStats (lines 265-283) which avoids this entirely.
items.estimated_impact: Up to M lazy loads where M = distinct expense categories per landlord.

items.title: Super-admin metrics cache key omits month-year — stale across boundaries
items.problem: Cache key `finance:superadmin:metrics` doesn't include month-year. After the month boundary, super-admin dashboard shows previous month's revenue for up to 5 minutes (TTL).
items.evidence: Line 86-88 — month/year are bound parameters but absent from cache key.
items.fix: Append month-year suffix: `rememberSuperAdminStats("metrics:{$month}:{$year}", ...)`. Manually invalidate the previous month's key on the 1st of each month.
items.estimated_impact: Removes 5-minute stale-data window after month rollover.

items.title: Missing index on payments.payment_date for monthly aggregations
items.problem: Existing indexes cover (landlord_id, payment_method) and (landlord_id, invoice_id). 12+ call sites use `whereMonth('payment_date', ...)` — MySQL can't use a function-based index for MONTH() without a generated column. Every monthly revenue query scans all payment rows for the landlord.
items.fix: Add migration: `$table->index(['landlord_id', 'payment_date'], 'payments_landlord_date_idx')`. Optionally create a covering composite index `(landlord_id, payment_date, amount)` for SUM aggregations.
items.estimated_impact: Converts table scan to range scan on payments. On 10k payment rows, drops from full-scan to small slice.

items.title: Missing composite index on notifications.(landlord_id, created_at) — filesort on every page
items.problem: NotificationsController::index and OperationsHubController::getNotificationsData both run `Notification::where('landlord_id', ..)->orderBy('created_at', 'desc')->paginate(20)`. Without (landlord_id, created_at) index, MySQL filesorts after filtering.
items.fix: Add migration: `$table->index(['landlord_id', 'created_at'], 'notifications_landlord_created_idx')`.
items.estimated_impact: Eliminates filesort on every notifications hub render.

items.title: users.landlord_id index relies on FK-implicit index (varies by engine)
items.problem: users.landlord_id is a foreignId. MySQL InnoDB auto-creates the FK index; SQLite does not. 25+ call sites use `User::where('landlord_id', ...)`. SQLite test runs may be slower because of missing index.
items.evidence: Base migration; FK constraint without explicit index.
items.fix: Add `$table->index(['landlord_id', 'role'], 'users_landlord_role_idx')` in a new migration. Benefits all engines uniformly.
items.estimated_impact: Standardizes performance across MySQL/SQLite envs; helps test parallelism.

phase_1.title: Critical Tenant-Isolation Fixes
phase_1.scope: SCOPE-S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6
phase_1.rationale: Highest blast radius; involves cross-tenant data exposure or write paths. Phase 3 cannot proceed until these land.
phase_1.estimated_effort: 1-2 days; each fix is small but requires regression tests.

phase_2.title: Critical Performance Fixes
phase_2.rationale: Hot-path N+1 affecting dashboards, schedulers, and bulk operations. Direct user impact.

phase_3.title: Defense-in-Depth Tenant Scope
phase_3.rationale: Functionally safe today, fragile to refactoring. Cleanup that also catches one MEDIUM-likely-IDOR (D3).

phase_4.title: Performance Optimization
phase_4.rationale: Real but cooler-path N+1 and cache opportunities. Should land before next high-traffic deployment.

phase_5.title: Cleanup & Consistency
phase_5.rationale: Pick up when touching the area. Includes 3 missing-index migrations worth running together.
phase_5.estimated_effort: Opportunistic; bundle migrations into one deployment window.

out_of_scope: Architectural refactor of TenantScope to lazy-resolve auth at query time (would fix the SCOPE-S* root cause but is a multi-week initiative)
Migration to repository pattern for all per-landlord queries (would address SCOPE-D4 systemically)
Conversion of PHPUnit suite to Pest (separate initiative)
Coverage threshold restoration (separate effort: backfill tests for legacy services to reach 70%)
Replacement of Eloquent with query builder for high-volume reports (consider when reports cross 1M rows)

metadata.audit_method: Two parallel feature-dev:code-explorer agents (multi-tenant scope + N+1/perf), exhaustive grep + LSP, no LLM speculation — every finding cites a file:line.
metadata.phase_1_2_priors: Phase 1 (32 bugs, commit 03e17b7): SQLi/auth/serialization/file-upload. Phase 2 (17 bugs, commit 4a305a0): data integrity. Phase 3 (45 findings, this PRD): scope leakage + N+1. Phase 4 candidates (future): observability gaps, queue idempotency, public-API rate limiting beyond /payment, secrets rotation.
